notes: 画質 chip double-tap-zoom — bullet-proof touch fix (2026-06). Re-reported: tapping the 画質 chip zooms the top button row, on iPad AND other touch devices, still happening after the <button> conversion + touch-action. Confirmed it does NOT reproduce with a desktop mouse click (the buttons don't change) — so it's a touch-browser gesture (iOS synthesises a double-tap zoom on this small control) that touch-action:manipulation didn't stop. Fix: the chip now owns its touch sequence — a touchend handler calls preventDefault() (which cancels the default touch gesture, i.e. the zoom, AND the synthesised click) and toggles the quality panel itself; the onclick attribute still drives desktop mouse, and drags are ignored. Verified via synthesised touch: touchend.defaultPrevented=true and the panel still opens. NOTE: needs a hard-reload on the device to pick up (bypass cache). Builds on v0.0.19.
